Name: kernel_optimization_for_low_latency_inference
Title: Kernel Optimization Techniques for Low-Latency Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By systematically optimizing specific CUDA kernel parameters and memory access patterns for common ML operations, we can achieve significant reductions in inference latency on commodity GPUs without sacrificing model accuracy.
Related Work: While existing literature focuses on unified frameworks for operator fusion and comparisons of GPU architectures, this proposal distinguishes itself by emphasizing practical, kernel-level optimizations tailored to specific operations (e.g., matrix multiplications, convolutions) in a reproducible benchmarking environment. The goal is to provide clear, actionable insights that can be deployed immediately in production systems.
Abstract: In the pursuit of real-time machine learning inference, optimizing kernel performance on commodity GPUs becomes crucial. This proposal outlines a series of systematic experiments aimed at refining CUDA kernel configurations for common ML operations, such as matrix multiplications and convolutions. By focusing on specific parameters such as tile sizes, memory access patterns, and thread block configurations, we will evaluate the impact of these optimizations on latency and throughput in a controlled environment. Our methodology emphasizes hardware-level profiling using tools like nvprof and Nsight to gather precise performance metrics. We will conduct a series of A/B latency races under fixed accuracy constraints, measuring p50/p99 latency and tokens per second across varying batch sizes. The anticipated outcome is a set of concrete, reproducible optimizations that demonstrate significant performance gains, providing direct value to practitioners aiming to enhance their ML deployment efficiency.
Experiments: 1. Baseline Measurement: Establish baseline latency and throughput metrics for common operations (e.g., matrix multiplication) using current CUDA implementations.
2. Tile Size Optimization: Test various tile sizes for matrix multiplication kernels, profiling performance to identify optimal configurations.
3. Memory Access Pattern Analysis: Implement cache-friendly memory access patterns to reduce memory latency and improve bandwidth utilization, measuring the impact on performance.
4. Thread Block Configuration: Experiment with different thread block configurations to maximize occupancy and minimize stalls, evaluating the effect on overall throughput.
5. Real-World Inference Scenarios: Apply the optimized kernels to real-world ML models (e.g., CNNs for image classification) and measure end-to-end inference latency and throughput.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include limited gains for certain operations due to inherent hardware limitations or saturation of optimization effects. The proposal may also face variability in performance based on the specific GPU model used. Furthermore, while the focus on kernel-level optimization is practical, it may not generalize well across all types of ML models or tasks.
